level_signals: Comp range $169K-$303K base (wide range suggests mid-to-senior levels; upper bound consistent with Staff/Senior)
Lead complex technical decision-making across platform systems
Define and drive developer platform strategy

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of product management experience working on highly technical products
Proven experience shipping developer tools, infrastructure products, or AI-powered technical systems
Strong technical depth: you can go toe-to-toe with engineers on architecture tradeoffs and system design
Clear systems thinking: you understand how complex components interact and can improve the whole, not just the parts
A track record of driving strategy and execution across ambiguous, fast-moving product areas

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building or designing web-based applications or developer-facing platforms
Experience working on AI agent systems, code generation tools, or programmable interfaces
Personal experience building and deploying web products (side projects, startups, or open-source work)
Familiarity with modern frontend frameworks, deployment pipelines, or cloud infrastructure
Experience scaling platform capabilities across multiple product teams

scope.surface: Figma Make Platform — code generation, execution runtimes, preview environments, deployments, observability, extensibility, and AI agent infrastructure
primary_metrics_inferred: Scale of agent infrastructure and eval system
core_responsibilities: Define and drive the developer platform strategy for Figma Make: code generation, editing, previewing, publishing, and integration with production-scale codebases
Create and execute agent infrastructure and eval system strategies to keep Make at the forefront of AI innovation
Translate Figma's AI agent roadmaps into clear platform requirements that enable scale, reliability, and extensibility
Lead complex technical decision-making across code generation, execution environments, agentic workflows, and deployment systems
Balance rapid feature velocity with long-term platform health
Engage directly with developers and technical customers and incorporate their requirements into strategy
Ship zero-to-one platform capabilities that unlock new product surfaces and enable GA milestones

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area (Make Platform)
Encourages applicants whose experience doesn't perfectly align
Comprehensive benefits package including equity, parental leave, learning stipend, and WFH stipend
Specific technical scope (runtimes, preview environments, deployments, observability) indicates a well-defined product area
red_flags: Very wide base salary range ($169K-$303K) makes it difficult to assess level or where a candidate might land
Remote pay is localized by 80-100% of range, which could significantly reduce compensation outside major hubs
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned — unclear policy
Required in-person onboarding for remote hires adds travel requirement not fully detailed

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: The base salary range is very wide ($169K-$303K), spanning multiple seniority levels. It is unclear whether this role is scoped as Senior IC or Staff PM.

JD states 'can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States' — remote-us is the correct classification but cities listed (SF, NY) are hub offices referenced only for pay transparency, not as required locations.
Nice-to-haves are listed under 'While it's not required, it's an added plus if you also have' — treated as soft/nice-to-have per rules.
